title: I compared my analysis vs a structured tool on the same trade… didn’t expect this
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
I took a trade recently that looked clean to me.

Good level.  
Decent structure.  
Felt confident.

Before entering, I ran it through a structured analysis tool I built.

It flagged something I completely ignored:

The invalidation didn’t make sense.

My stop was basically… random.

That’s when it hit me.

Most of my bad trades weren’t about direction.

They were about unclear logic.

* entry based on “feeling”
* stop placed for comfort
* no real invalidation

So now I do something simple:

Before every trade → I force a structured check:

* where is the level
* where is the entry
* where is the invalidation

If I can’t answer clearly → no trade.

That alone removed a lot of weak setups.

I built a small tool to do this automatically from a chart screenshot.

Acts like a second pair of eyes.

Curious if anyone else here uses something similar.

If you want to see how it works:  
[https://herculmarket.com](https://herculmarket.com/)
